title: gavilea lutea
intro: Gavilea lutea, also known as the Golden Gavilea, is a flowering plant species native to New Zealand. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including its ability to soothe coughs and alleviate congestion. Gavilea lutea can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in traditional Maori medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
